message:
feat(core): extend asupersync runtime compat with helper abstractions (round 2)

Add cfg-gated helper functions that abstract over API differences between
tokio and the asupersync custom runtime, replacing inline cfg blocks with
callable wrappers:

runtime.rs:
- watch_has_changed(): abstracts tokio's fallible has_changed() (returns
  Result<bool>) vs asupersync's infallible variant (returns bool directly)
- watch_borrow_and_update_clone(): abstracts tokio's borrow_and_update()
  followed by clone() vs asupersync's single borrow_and_clone() method
- mpsc_max_capacity(): abstracts tokio's max_capacity() vs asupersync's
  capacity() for obtaining the channel's fixed upper bound
- mpsc_recv_option(): abstracts tokio's recv() -> Option<T> vs
  asupersync's recv(&cx) -> Result<T> (converting to Option via .ok())
- Replace all inline has_changed/borrow_and_update/max_capacity/recv
  call sites in the config-update hot paths (main loop, tailer spawn,
  capture ingress, pattern reload) with the new helpers
- Fix RuntimeHandle::capture_queue_depth to use saturating_sub for
  robustness when capacity transiently exceeds max_capacity

storage.rs:
- Extend WriteCommandSender::max_capacity() with cfg-gated body that
  dispatches to inner.capacity() under asupersync vs inner.max_capacity()
  under tokio, matching the different mpsc::Sender APIs

tailer.rs:
- Add cfg-gated permit reservation blocks in the poll_pane capture path:
  asupersync's tx.reserve(&cx) requires an explicit context parameter
  while tokio's tx.reserve() does not
- Apply to both the overflow-gap codepath and the normal capture codepath,
  preserving identical PollOutcome::Backpressure / ChannelClosed semantics

These helpers reduce boilerplate and make future runtime-divergent call
sites easier to maintain as the asupersync migration progresses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
